text:
Source type: Paper topic

Topic: Foundational ML

Category: Machine Learning

Subtitle: Representation learning, embeddings, self-supervision, and multimodal pretraining

Description: A literature map for foundational machine learning papers covering representation learning, word embeddings, autoencoders, contrastive learning, self-supervised vision, multimodal pretraining, and information-theoretic foundations.

Intro:
This topic starts from information-theoretic and autoencoder foundations, then moves into word embeddings, broad representation learning, contrastive learning, self-supervised visual models, masked autoencoders, and multimodal pretraining.
The curated set is designed as a compact foundation layer for applied ML readers who want to understand the papers behind modern embeddings, representation learning, and pretraining workflows.

Map intro: This map focuses on foundational machine learning literature. Horizontal position reflects publication year and vertical bands reflect placeholder citation groups.

Papers:
- Efficient Estimation of Word Representations in Vector Space (2013)
- GloVe: Global Vectors for Word Representation (2014)
- Reducing the Dimensionality of Data with Neural Networks (2006)
- Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes (2013)
- Representation Learning: A Review and New Perspectives (2013)
- Representation Learning with Contrastive Predictive Coding (2018)
- A Simple Framework for Contrastive Learning of Visual Representations (2020)
- Momentum Contrast for Unsupervised Visual Representation Learning (2020)
- Bootstrap Your Own Latent: A New Approach to Self-Supervised Learning (2020)
- Masked Autoencoders Are Scalable Vision Learners (2022)

- Deep Learning (2015)

title: Limit Order Books & HFT

text:
Source type: Paper topic

Topic: Limit Order Books & HFT

Category: Financial ML

Subtitle: LOB-HFT: limit order books, deep learning, and high-frequency market prediction

Description: A literature map for limit order book forecasting, market microstructure signals, benchmark datasets, and deep learning architectures for high-frequency trading research.

Intro:
This topic starts from market microstructure and public datasets such as LOBSTER and FI-2010, then moves into CNN, LSTM, attention, transformer, normalization, and benchmark studies.
The map uses publication year on the horizontal axis and placeholder citation groups on the vertical axis so the visual structure reflects both chronology and impact class.
The right panel shows paper-level details and can later be extended with abstracts, notes, Zotero links, tags, or custom project annotations.

title: Real Estate Forecasting
description: A literature map for real estate forecasting and appraisal across index construction, macroeconomic drivers, sentiment, multimodal fusion, and satellite-based contextual modeling.
text:
Source type: Paper topic

Topic: Real Estate Forecasting

Category: Financial ML

Subtitle: Indices, macro drivers, urban ML, multimodal fusion, and satellite-informed forecasting

Description: A literature map for real estate forecasting and appraisal across index construction, macroeconomic drivers, sentiment, multimodal fusion, and satellite-based contextual modeling.

Intro:
This topic combines classical real estate forecasting and property price index research with modern machine learning, multimodal appraisal, and spatiotemporal urban modeling.
The curated set excludes implementation-only software citations and focuses on domain papers, methodological works that are directly relevant, surveys, and datasets that shape the research narrative.
It is designed to support work on sub-city or index-level forecasting, multimodal context fusion, sentiment-driven signals, macro variables, and built-up area proxies derived from remote sensing.
